name: French Defence
description: Solid e6/d5 counterplay: Advance, Winawer, Tarrasch, Exchange and KIA.
source: curated repertoire (scripts/repertoires/french.pgn) + lichess explorer, ratings 1400/1600/1800, speeds blitz/rapid

children.comment: Loosening - but it is White's only way to unpin the position.
Ignore the bait: via f5 this knight attacks d4 a fourth time. Bxh6 just opens the g-file for Black.
Same idea: the knight comes to f5 and White must watch d4 forever.
Adding a third attacker on d4 and eyeing b2 for free.
Hit the chain at its base immediately; every tempo matters before White consolidates.
Brave and correct at club level: the attack looks scarier than it is, and Black's queenside play is faster.
Hit c3 while White is deciding where the king goes.
The Exchange Winawer holds no terror: develop actively and use the open e-file first.
Keep this bishop: it points at h2 for the rest of the game.
The isolated pawn is a small price for free piece play - every Black piece has a natural square.
Trade the bishops: the d3 bishop is White's only proud piece.
Better than ...Nf6: this knight can reroute to g6 or f5 and never blocks the f-pawn.
The active square: the bishop and a later ...Qf6 or ...Bg4 give Black the initiative in the "drawish" Exchange.
Covers e5: White's central lever loses its sting, and ...b5-b4 rolls on the other wing.
Point the bishop at the future g3/h2 targets before White's setup is ready.

variations.name: Advance
variations.intro: 3.e5 closes the centre - so attack its base. Everything Black plays aims at d4: ...c5, ...Nc6, ...Qb6, and the h6 knight heading for f5.

variations.name: Winawer
variations.intro: 3.Nc3 meets the pin 3...Bb4: give up the bishop, wreck White's pawns, and race on the queenside while White attacks the king.

variations.name: Tarrasch
variations.intro: 3.Nd2 avoids the pin but blocks the c1 bishop. Open the position with ...c5 and develop faster than White untangles.

variations.name: Exchange
variations.intro: 3.exd5 tries to bore Black to death. Refuse: develop with small asymmetries and outplay the position piece by piece.

variations.name: King's Indian Attack
variations.intro: 2.d3 announces the KIA. Take the centre White declined, develop behind it, and meet the kingside pawn storm with the thematic ...f6.
